notes: Seed pack: foundation + mechanical starter concepts. Status: validated for basic definitions; draft where nuance is venue-specific.
Canonical memory for the system — not LLM output. Changes via PR/review only.

concepts.name: Price
concepts.definition: Last traded price or mid quote for an instrument at a point in time.
concepts.trader_meaning: What you think you pay or receive per unit.
concepts.why_it_matters: Sizing, PnL, and comparisons across venues depend on a clear price reference.
concepts.execution_impact: Limit price selection and fill expectations when execution is allowed.
failure_modes: Confusing mark vs last in illiquid books

concepts.name: Bid
concepts.definition: The highest price a buyer is willing to pay for an asset at a given moment.
concepts.trader_meaning: Buy-side quote you hit when selling into the book.
concepts.why_it_matters: Tells you immediate sell-side liquidity quality.
concepts.decision_impact: Influences where you place limits relative to the market.
concepts.execution_impact: Sell market orders lift bids; limits join bid side.
failure_modes: Treating stale bid as guaranteed size

concepts.name: Ask (Offer)
concepts.definition: The lowest price a seller will accept for an asset at a given moment.
concepts.trader_meaning: Sell-side quote you hit when buying from the book.
concepts.why_it_matters: Defines the cost to lift liquidity now.
concepts.decision_impact: Buy decisions reference ask for immediate fills.
concepts.execution_impact: Buy market orders lift asks; limits join ask side.
failure_modes: Ignoring ask size when it is tiny

concepts.name: Bid–Ask Spread
concepts.definition: The difference between best ask and best bid (ask minus bid).
concepts.trader_meaning: Tight spread usually means cheaper to trade now; wide spread means friction.
concepts.why_it_matters: Direct read on liquidity and transaction cost before fees.
concepts.decision_impact: Wider spread often implies smaller size or more caution.
concepts.execution_impact: May favor limits or smaller clips to reduce crossing cost.
examples: Spread is 2 cents on a 86 dollar name.

concepts.name: Market Order
concepts.definition: An instruction to buy or sell immediately at the best available prices until filled or capped.
concepts.trader_meaning: I will pay whatever the book requires right now to get done.
concepts.why_it_matters: Certainty of execution vs uncertain price.
concepts.decision_impact: Use when urgency dominates price precision.
concepts.execution_impact: Crosses spread; may walk the book on size.
examples: Market buy lifts through multiple ask levels.

concepts.name: Limit Order
concepts.definition: An instruction to buy only at or below a price, or sell only at or above a price.
concepts.trader_meaning: I will not pay worse than this (buy) or accept less than this (sell).
concepts.why_it_matters: Controls worst-case price; may not fill.
concepts.execution_impact: Rests on book as maker liquidity when price allows.
failure_modes: Forgot to cancel stale limits in fast markets
examples: Buy limit at 85.50 while best ask is 86.

concepts.name: Volume
concepts.definition: The amount of the asset traded over a time window (often base-asset units or quote notional).
concepts.trader_meaning: How much traded recently — activity and participation.
concepts.why_it_matters: Context for whether moves are on thin or thick trade flow.
concepts.decision_impact: High volume can validate moves; low volume warns of noise.
concepts.execution_impact: Impacts how much size can clear without large impact.
failure_modes: Comparing venues with different volume definitions

concepts.name: Liquidity
concepts.definition: The ability to trade size near current prices without moving the price excessively.
concepts.trader_meaning: Can I get in and out without paying the earth?
concepts.why_it_matters: Core to feasibility of strategy and risk of slippage.
concepts.decision_impact: Influences venue choice and max comfortable size.
concepts.execution_impact: Drives order type and clip size when executing.
failure_modes: Assuming infinite liquidity at top of book
examples: Only 500 units within 10 bps of mid.

concepts.name: Candlestick (OHLC Bar)
concepts.definition: A time-bucket summary: open, high, low, close (and often volume).
concepts.trader_meaning: How price evolved over a bar on a chart.
concepts.why_it_matters: Trend and range visualization; timeframe choice matters.
concepts.decision_impact: Timeframe selection frames noise vs signal.
concepts.execution_impact: Not execution directly — context for timing.
failure_modes: Look-ahead bias in backtests using close within bar

concepts.name: Timeframe
concepts.definition: The duration of each candle or aggregation window (e.g. 1m, 15m, 1d).
concepts.trader_meaning: The zoom level on the chart and signal speed.
concepts.why_it_matters: Same market can look trending on one timeframe and choppy on another.
concepts.decision_impact: Aligns indicators and rules to decision horizon.
concepts.execution_impact: Usually indirect; affects when signals fire.
failure_modes: Mixing signals from mismatched timeframes without rules
examples: Use 4h structure for bias, 15m for entries.

concepts.name: Slippage
concepts.definition: Difference between expected fill price and actual average fill price.
concepts.trader_meaning: How much worse (or better) the fill vs the quote you saw.
concepts.why_it_matters: Dominates small-edge strategies and realistic backtests.
concepts.execution_impact: May require limits, TWAP, or smaller clips.

concepts.name: Order Book Depth
concepts.definition: Quantity available to trade at and near current prices across price levels.
concepts.trader_meaning: How much is actually for sale or bid under the surface.
concepts.why_it_matters: Thin depth implies large price impact for modest size.
concepts.decision_impact: Warns when size is large vs visible liquidity.

concepts.name: Price Impact
concepts.definition: The adverse price movement caused by your own order relative to pre-trade mid.
concepts.trader_meaning: How much I move the market against myself when I trade.
concepts.why_it_matters: Separates trading skill and algo quality from directional alpha.
concepts.decision_impact: Caps realistic size for a strategy.
concepts.execution_impact: Drives scheduling and passive vs aggressive routing.
examples: Buying 1% of ADV moves price more than 5 bps.

concepts.name: Volatility
concepts.definition: The magnitude of price fluctuations over time (statistical or heuristic).
concepts.trader_meaning: How wild the ride is — risk and opportunity scale.
concepts.why_it_matters: Sizing, stops, and options-like intuition depend on vol regime.
concepts.decision_impact: Higher vol often implies smaller size or wider risk buffers.
concepts.execution_impact: Wider stops or slower execution may be needed in fast tape.

concepts.name: Maker / Taker
concepts.definition: Maker orders add liquidity to the book; taker orders remove liquidity by crossing the spread.
concepts.trader_meaning: Am I getting paid rebate as a maker or paying spread as a taker?
concepts.why_it_matters: Fee structure and adverse selection differ by role.
concepts.decision_impact: Strategy choice between passive quotes vs aggressive fills.
concepts.execution_impact: Post-only limits vs market — different fees and risks.
failure_modes: Assuming maker fill when you actually took
examples: Post-only buy rests below best ask — maker if filled.
